data.title: Sprint Data
data.noSprints: No sprints yet. Add your first sprint above.

velocity.title: Velocity Chart
velocity.explainer: Velocity is the amount of work a team completes per sprint. Track it over time to understand your team's sustainable pace. Use the rolling average (last 3-5 sprints) for planning, not the peak.
velocity.noData: Add at least 2 sprints to see the velocity chart.

burndown.title: Sprint Burn-Down
burndown.explainer: Burn-Down shows remaining work over time within a sprint. The ideal line assumes linear progress. Deviations reveal planning issues (planned too much) or impediments (blocked mid-sprint).
burndown.noData: Add sprint data to see burn-down.

burnup.title: Release Burn-Up
burnup.explainer: Burn-Up shows cumulative completed work vs. total scope over all sprints. Unlike burn-down, it makes scope changes visible. When the completed line meets the scope line, you're done.
burnup.noData: Add at least 2 sprints to see burn-up.

forecast.title: Release Forecast
forecast.explainer: Forecast uses your rolling velocity to estimate how many sprints until the backlog is done. It shows a range (optimistic/average/pessimistic) based on velocity variance — because point estimates lie.
forecast.noData: Add at least 3 sprints with completions to generate a forecast.
